steps: Since $\overline{LM} \parallel \overline{PQ}$, alternate interior angles give $\angle LMR = \angle QPR$ and $\angle MLR = \angle PQR$. By AA similarity, $\triangle LMR \sim \triangle PQR$ with $MR$ corresponding to $RP$.
The linear scale factor from $\triangle LMR$ to $\triangle PQR$ is $k = \frac{RP}{MR} = \frac{14}{8} = \frac{7}{4}$. Areas scale by $k^2 = \frac{49}{16}$.

Test all four choices against $\text{Area}(\triangle PQR) = 36 \times \frac{49}{16} = \frac{1764}{16} = \frac{441}{4}$:
  • Choice A — '$\frac{576}{49}$': This equals $36 \times \frac{16}{49}$, which would be the area if $\triangle PQR$ were the smaller triangle (ratio inverted). ✗
  • Choice B — '$\frac{144}{7}$': Approximately 20.6. Does not match $\frac{441}{4} = 110.25$. ✗
  • Choice C — '63': $36 \times \frac{7}{4} = 63$ uses the linear scale factor instead of the area scale factor. ✗
  • Choice D — '$\frac{441}{4}$': $36 \times \left(\frac{7}{4}\right)^2 = 36 \times \frac{49}{16} = \frac{441}{4}$. Correct area scaling. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
